message:
chore(beads): close ft-youhf, ft-mdaon, ft-arhlm, ft-m1zkw — all already fixed

- ft-youhf: CxPolicyInjector API drift fixed by e28bb88d
- ft-mdaon: CSI MR + concatenation property fixed by db835e7b
- ft-arhlm: MCP golden regeneration command fixed by f6e7cc15+
- ft-m1zkw: PaneNode recursive Split proptests added by fef393d1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-akx00.7.3","title":"Implement SFTP metadata mutation in libssh file wrappers","description":"`frankenterm/ssh/src/filewrap.rs` still leaves SFTP metadata mutation unimplemented.

This task owns making metadata-setting behavior real for the supported libssh-backed file path, including permissions/timestamps or any intentionally supported subset.","design":"1. Define the supported metadata mutation surface for this SSH layer.
2. Implement the underlying libssh/SFTP call path and error mapping.
3. Ensure partial support is explicit if some metadata classes are not portable.
4. Add tests against fixtures or controlled remote endpoints for success and failure behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Metadata mutation is no longer left as an unimplemented placeholder.
- Supported metadata fields and limitations are documented.
- Errors are mapped clearly instead of collapsing into generic unsupported behavior.
- Tests verify real mutation where supported.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Keep the scope honest: a smaller explicit set of mutable metadata is better than pretending to support everything POSIX can express.

## Verification Baseline
- Follow the shared verification and artifact contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCastle","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:26.525184Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T21:40:43.111733Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T21:40:43.111392Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:26.525184Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2770,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"This is a classic “tiny missing feature” that quietly blocks real workflows. The bead should leave future users knowing exactly which metadata operations do and do not work over SSH.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:26Z"},{"id":2796,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan
- Proposed unit tests:
  - metadata mapping to the underlying SSH/SFTP API
  - partial-support and error translation behavior
- Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_sftp_metadata_mutation.sh`
  - mutate metadata on controlled remote files and verify before/after state
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - remote host marker, path, fields mutated, before/after stats, command transcript, redacted remote logs","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.7.4","title":"Implement SSH Match Exec evaluation in config loading","description":"`frankenterm/ssh/src/config.rs` still leaves SSH `Match Exec` criteria unimplemented.

This task owns deciding and implementing the project’s real behavior for `Match Exec` so SSH config parsing no longer silently omits an advertised criterion.","design":"1. Confirm the desired compatibility target for SSH config semantics in this project.
2. Implement `Match Exec` evaluation with clear execution, quoting, and error rules.
3. Harden the design against obvious safety pitfalls from arbitrary command execution.
4. Add tests for matching, non-matching, command failure, and disabled-policy cases.","acceptance_criteria":"- `Match Exec` is no longer a placeholder omission.
- The config loader documents the exact supported semantics and any safety restrictions.
- Tests cover success, mismatch, failure, and disabled/denied execution modes.
- Callers can tell whether a config line was unsupported, denied, or simply evaluated false.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Be explicit about security posture here. If command execution must be gated or disabled by policy, document and implement that as part of the feature rather than bolting it on later.

## Verification Baseline
- Follow the shared verification and artifact contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCastle","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27.135986Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:52:53.816310Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:52:53.816104Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27.135986Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2771,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"`Match Exec` is one of those features where naïve compatibility can create security regret. The clever implementation is the one that is both honest and safe.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27Z"},{"id":2797,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan
- Proposed unit tests:
  - parsing and evaluation of `Match Exec`
  - quoting, exit-code semantics, and disabled-policy behavior
- Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_ssh_match_exec.sh`
  - run config fixtures with allowed, failing, and denied `Match Exec` commands
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - match clause id, command executed or denied, exit code, policy gate result, config fixture snapshot","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.8","title":"Define shared verification, E2E, and artifact contract for placeholder-remediation work","description":"Define the shared verification contract for the entire `ft-akx00` program so implementers do not improvise test coverage, logging shape, artifact capture, or execution evidence on a bead-by-bead basis.

This bead exists because placeholder-remediation is high risk: many changes touch trust-sensitive control paths, platform backends, or user-facing UX surfaces. We want one reusable contract for proof, not 24 inconsistent interpretations of “tested”.","design":"1. Define the minimum unit-test standard for this program: happy path, edge/boundary behavior, explicit failure/error/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability/idempotence where relevant.
2. Define the E2E/integration contract: each concrete bead must land either a dedicated script or an explicitly named extension to an existing shared suite.
3. Define the structured logging schema for verification runs. Minimum fields: `bead_id`, `scenario_id`, `surface`, `step`, `status`, `duration_ms`, `correlation_id`, `backend`, `platform`, `artifact_dir`, and redaction markers.
4. Define the artifact manifest for every verification run. Minimum files: `commands.txt`, `env.txt`, `structured.log`, `stdout.txt`, `stderr.txt`, `summary.json`, plus surface-specific artifacts like screenshots, pixel diffs, JSON payload snapshots, or remote traces.
5. Define naming conventions for new E2E scripts and artifact directories so failures are easy to browse.
6. Require heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark workloads to run via `rch exec -- ...`, with worker identity and command transcript captured in evidence.","acceptance_criteria":"- The shared verification contract is explicit enough that every concrete `ft-akx00.*.*` task can reference it without ambiguity.
- Proposed E2E suite names and artifact directory conventions exist for every track in the program.
- The contract includes structured logging, artifact capture, redaction, and reproduction-command requirements.
- The contract is reflected back into the existing `ft-akx00` epics/tasks via updated acceptance criteria, notes, or comments.","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.480153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.479984Z","close_reason":"Completed shared verification contract","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2773,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Suggested suite map for future implementers:
- machine-control/client/sdk -> `scripts/e2e_machine_control_*.sh`
- operator/startup/backup -> `scripts/e2e_ops_*.sh`
- connector/search -> `scripts/e2e_search_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_connector_*.sh`
- core/FTUI -> `scripts/e2e_core_*.sh` and TUI snapshot fixtures
- rendering -> `scripts/e2e_render_*.sh` plus screenshot/pixel-diff artifacts
- mux/SSH -> `scripts/e2e_mux_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_ssh_*.sh`

Completion evidence for any future bead under this epic should be considered incomplete unless it records exact commands, artifact paths, and enough logs to diagnose failure without rerunning locally.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25Z"},{"id":2821,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Progress update: tightened the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` and the owned ft-akx00 harnesses for 6.2 / 6.4. The concrete changes are: 1) explicit probe/smoke log path helpers, 2) extractor helpers for selected worker / probe worker inventory / sync duration / remote duration / exit code, 3) a sentinel smoke log when `RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT=1`, and 4) owned harness summaries now record `rch_probe`, `rch_smoke`, and per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars. Verified this contract concretely with `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_2_backend_resize_framebuffer.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.2/backend_resize_framebuffer/20260403T182413Z`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:28:32Z"},{"id":2822,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Follow-up validation: the tightened contract was also exercised via `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_4_terminfo_image_rendering.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.4/terminfo_image_rendering/20260403T182902Z`. That run produced a complete `summary.json` plus `rch_probe` / `rch_smoke` / per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars even on failure. Results were 3 pass / 1 fail; the failing leg was remote clippy, and the failure was unrelated repo lint debt in `frankenterm/dynamic/derive/src/attr.rs` (`clippy::collapsible_match`), not a break in the contract plumbing itself. This gives the ft-akx00 contract both an honest failure-path example and a passing-step example with captured worker identity and durations.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:36:45Z"},{"id":3524,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"SunnyTiger","text":"SunnyTiger 2026-04-19: standardized the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in repo docs. Canonical references are now `docs/test-logging-contract.md` (new `ft-akx00` profile with required JSONL fields, artifact set, and reproduction-command rules) and `docs/e2e-harness-spec.md` (new bead-scoped verification profile, required artifact root, suite map for tracks 2-7, and redaction/evidence requirements). Also extended `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` metadata sidecars to record `repo_root`, `smoke_target_dir`, timeout settings, and whether smoke preflight was explicitly requested to be skipped, so the docs match auditable on-disk evidence instead of chat-only expectations.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:32:54Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-arhlm","title":"[MEDIUM] MCP missing-golden panic still points operators at the wrong regeneration command","description":"Commit f6e7cc15 corrected the drift-path regeneration command, but the missing-golden panic still tells operators to run `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test --test mcp_manifest_golden` (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:200-205`). In this workspace, default features enable a much wider surface than the minimal review lane, so regenerating via that command can produce a different manifest than the intended `--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` path. This leaves the test with two conflicting operator instructions and can regenerate the wrong golden when the fixture is absent. Expected fix: make the missing-file panic use the same explicit feature-set command as the drift path and top-of-file documentation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:05.098815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T17:58:09.567969Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T17:58:09.567532Z","close_reason":"Already resolved at HEAD. Commit 1bde42b3 (ft-nnfwn: freeze no-db mcp manifest surface) rewrote the missing-golden panic path through a shared read_or_update_golden helper; both panic branches now embed the full regeneration command with explicit flags — the missing-file panic at lines 172-177 (UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test --test mcp_manifest_golden --no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime) and the drift panic at lines 189-197 carry the identical command. Top-of-file doc block at lines 13-14 also matches. grep 'cargo test --test mcp_manifest_golden' returns 3 occurrences, all with --no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime, so operator instructions are consistent.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-arhlm","title":"[MEDIUM] MCP missing-golden panic still points operators at the wrong regeneration command","description":"Commit f6e7cc15 corrected the drift-path regeneration command, but the missing-golden panic still tells operators to run `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test --test mcp_manifest_golden` (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:200-205`). In this workspace, default features enable a much wider surface than the minimal review lane, so regenerating via that command can produce a different manifest than the intended `--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` path. This leaves the test with two conflicting operator instructions and can regenerate the wrong golden when the fixture is absent. Expected fix: make the missing-file panic use the same explicit feature-set command as the drift path and top-of-file documentation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:05.098815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:02.105111Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:02.104627Z","close_reason":"Already fixed — all three panic messages (header doc, missing-golden, drift) consistently use --no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime after f6e7cc15 and subsequent commits","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b5rbd","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:04.554054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945504Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945232Z","close_reason":"dup of ft-htf0i","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b677n","title":"[MEDIUM] wgpu readback poll loop has no test coverage for timeout, disconnect, or error-propagation paths","description":"Commit 6f41130c (br-43bbl fix wgpu readback polling) rewrites the
readback wait in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193
from:

\`\`\`rust
self.device.poll(PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) })?;
receiver.recv_timeout(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT)?...
\`\`\`

to a bounded poll loop:

\`\`\`rust
let deadline = Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT;
loop {
    self.device.poll(wgpu::PollType::Poll)?;
    match receiver.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) {
        Ok(result) => { result?; break; }
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) if Instant::now() < deadline => continue,
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => return Err(...),
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => return Err(...),
    }
}
\`\`\`

Concerns with the new implementation:

1. \`PollType::Poll\` is non-blocking by contract in wgpu 25. The loop
   relies on \`Poll\` actually driving the \`map_async\` callback forward.
   If a future wgpu point release changes \`Poll\` to be strictly
   \"dispatch-only\" (no callback invocation), the loop would always
   hit the 5s timeout. There is no test pinning this behavior.

2. The \`Disconnected\` branch is reachable only if the \`map_async\`
   closure drops without sending — that happens e.g., if wgpu drops
   the submission without invoking the callback (device lost, internal
   error). No test exercises this path.

3. \`Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT\` panics on overflow
   (impossible in practice for 5s, but worth noting as a latent
   concern). Prefer \`checked_add\`.

4. Busy-wait cost: on the timeout path, the loop spins ~500 iterations
   (5000ms / 10ms) calling \`device.poll\` each time. The prior
   \`PollType::Wait\` blocked in the driver. This is a CPU-efficiency
   regression on cancellation/device-loss paths, not a correctness
   regression.

Impact: readback behavior on wgpu failure paths (device lost, long
delay, map_async disconnect) is silently unverified. A correctness
regression in \`PollType::Poll\` semantics between wgpu versions would
silently break screenshots and readback-based features.

Fix direction:
- Add a unit-level test with a mock/stub Device that controls
  \`poll()\` return timing and can force a Disconnected on the mpsc
  channel. Assert each of the four loop outcomes.
- Consider moving the readback loop into a small helper function
  that takes a \`Fn()\` poll closure + channel receiver, to make it
  unit-testable without a real GPU.
- If unit isolation is impractical, at minimum add an integration
  test that triggers a readback and asserts completion within a
  tight CPU budget (regression test for busy-wait blow-up).

Severity: medium — latent risk in a GPU-facing path that typically
works and has no automated coverage for failure modes.

Evidence: commit 6f41130c diff;
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193 at HEAD.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062383Z","close_reason":"Added timeout/disconnect/cancel-style readback coverage and hardened the helper deadline with checked_add","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","review","test-gap","testing","webgpu"]}
 {"id":"ft-cbeyl","title":"[MEDIUM] mcp_manifest_golden db-gated tool whitelist silently drifts when tool renamed or removed","description":"In commit 1bde42b3 (ft-nnfwn: freeze no-db mcp manifest surface),
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:254-316 hard-codes
three whitelists of tools/resources/templates expected to be db-gated:

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [
    \"wa.accounts\",
    \"wa.events\",
    \"wa.reserve\",
    \"wa.release\",
    \"wa.search\",
    \"wa.send\",
    \"wa.workflow_run\",
] {
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

The assertion only checks absence from \`no_db_tools\`. If a tool is
renamed (e.g., \`wa.accounts\` → \`wa.list_accounts\`) or removed entirely,
the whitelist entry silently stays true (\`!no_db_tools.contains(\"wa.accounts\")\`
is trivially true when \`wa.accounts\` no longer exists in either manifest).

The same drift pattern applies to the resource whitelist
(lines ~285, \`wa://accounts\`, \`wa://events\`, \`wa://reservations\`) and
the template whitelist (lines ~295, \`wa://accounts/{service}\` etc.).

Impact: the no-db subset guard gives false confidence. A tool could
accidentally become always-on (or be removed from db-gated registration)
without the test catching it.

Fix: for each whitelist entry, assert \`full_tools.contains(entry)\`
BEFORE checking \`!no_db_tools.contains(entry)\`. That way, if the
whitelisted tool disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test
fails and forces whoever made the change to update the whitelist
deliberately.

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [...] {
    assert!(
        full_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"whitelist drift: {expected_absent_tool} no longer exists in db-backed manifest — update the test\"
    );
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

Severity rationale: medium — test-quality issue that masks regressions
in MCP tool surface categorization. Not a correctness bug in production
code.

Evidence: commit 1bde42b3 diff lines 262-316 of the new
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` test body.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915565Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915363Z","close_reason":"Added full_*.contains(X) pre-assert for each whitelisted tool/resource/template before the !no_db.contains(X) check. If a whitelisted identifier disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test now fails instead of passing trivially. Fix landed in 4d52f550. Verified 6/6 tests pass via rch exec.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","test-gap","testing"]}
@@ -69,8 +69,8 @@
 {"id":"ft-kfkyi","title":"DistributedHttpClient must not transparently follow 3xx redirects","description":"# Security: DistributedHttpClient transparent redirect following

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 verification work. `DistributedHttpClient`
(built on the default asupersync HTTP client config) **follows 3xx redirects
transparently** by default. A 302 with an arbitrary `Location:` header is
followed without caller visibility.

Current behavior is pinned by `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot`
in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs` (tests module) — the test
asserts `Err` on a 302 whose Location is unresolvable, and explicitly
documents that assertion flipping is the trigger for this bead's work.

## Security concern
`DistributedHttpClient` is used for distributed node-to-node RPC (health
checks, event forwarding). Peer URLs are supposed to point at trusted
nodes. Transparent redirect following means:

- A compromised peer can respond `302 Location: http://attacker.com/` →
  next request body is sent to attacker (including event data, pane
  captures, auth tokens).
- Even without compromise, a misconfigured peer with a redirect loop
  could cause unbounded work.

## Fix
Configure `RedirectPolicy::none()` at the `DistributedHttpClient`
construction site — see `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`
around `DistributedHttpClient::new()` and `DistributedHttpClient::plaintext()`.

This forces the caller to explicitly handle (or ignore) the redirect.

## Verification
After the fix:
1. `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot` will
   fail — update it to assert `resp.status == 302` and rename to drop
   the "current-behavior-snapshot" suffix. Rename to
   `distributed_http_client_returns_3xx_redirect_as_ok_response`.
2. Add a second test asserting that a 302 → a different internal host
   is ALSO observable (not followed) — use a second TcpListener in
   the test so the "Location" resolves but the client still doesn't
   follow it.

## Out of scope
- Building a full redirect-following mechanism (if callers want redirects,
  they can opt in explicitly by following the Location themselves after
  inspecting it).
- The asupersync HTTP client's default RedirectPolicy — that's a library
  default and changing it would affect every other caller. The fix is
  to override at the FrankenTerm distributed-client construction site.

## References
- Finding tick: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 (commit 4013f6e9)
- Related doc: docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5 (out of scope carve-out)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:46.872931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852811Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852502Z","close_reason":"Completed by tick 351 (commit 4717c434): DistributedHttpClient::new() now constructs via builder with .no_redirects() explicitly. 3xx responses are Ok(Response{status: 3xx}) observable to caller. Snapshot test renamed + flipped to assert Ok(302).  {"id":"ft-l9mxa","title":"DistributedHttpClient response-read path does not observe mid-flight cx-cancel","description":"# DistributedHttpClient mid-flight cx-cancel gap

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 verification work. When a caller fires
`cx.cancel_with(...)` WHILE a `DistributedHttpClient::get` is in flight (blocked
reading response bytes from a stalled server), the cancel does NOT propagate to
the HTTP client's read loop.

## Reproduction
The ft-xbnl0.2.4 test
`distributed::tests::distributed_http_client_mid_flight_cancel_does_not_hang`
exercises this. Observed behavior:

    elapsed = 10.02s
    result = Err("deadline has elapsed at Time(10000000041ns)")

The elapsed = ~10s matches the test's DEFENSIVE outer timeout ceiling
(`crate::runtime_compat::timeout(10s, client.get(...))`), not the 50 ms
cx-cancel fire. The cancel went through (cx.is_cancel_requested() would return
true) but the HTTP client's await points didn't observe it.

## Relation to other findings
- **Tick 328** documented that `timeout_with_cx` observes cx *budget deadline*
  but not direct cx *cancel*. This is the same shape of gap at a different
  layer — the HTTP client wraps asupersync::http::h1 whose await points
  don't poll cx.
- **ft-kfkyi** (closed tick 351) was a distinct concern (3xx redirect
  exfil). This finding is lower-severity: not a security issue, just an
  operational-ergonomics gap.

## Impact
- Hung distributed RPC calls will eventually time out via internal limits
  (if any configured) but won't observe operator-initiated cancel.
- Agent workflows that fire cx.cancel to abort a slow peer call will wait
  the full internal-timeout window before the call returns.

## Fix approach (candidate paths)
1. Extend `DistributedHttpClient::get/post` to race the inner future against
   a cx-cancel future (tokio::select! or similar race).
2. Patch asupersync::http::h1 to thread cx through its await points and
   honor cancel at read/write boundaries. Bigger scope; upstream change.
3. Wrap the DistributedHttpClient inner calls in `timeout_with_cx` —
   but per tick 328, that only observes budget, not cancel. So this
   doesn't help unless the caller sets a short budget.

Option (1) is the narrowest change; aligns with the cx-first migration
pattern and doesn't require upstream asupersync work.

## Verification
The existing tick-380 snapshot test is the flip trigger. When the fix lands:
1. Observed elapsed drops from ~10s to << 10s (cx-cancel observed mid-read).
2. `result` shape changes from the outer-timeout Err to the HTTP client's
   own cancel-shaped Err.
3. Update the snapshot assertions to require the faster behavior.

## References
- Finding: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 (commit 4d925607)
- Related: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5` out-of-scope carve-out
  for primitive-doc-only coverage (timeout_with_cx / sleep_with_cx — ticks
  328 / 331).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:43.612639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342659Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 387 (commit 99640e41): DistributedHttpClient::{get,post} now race inner call against cx-cancel watcher (50ms poll). Mid-flight cancel surfaces as Ok(Err(Cancelled)) in ~70ms. Tick-380 snapshot test tightened to assert the new fast-cancel behavior (elapsed < 1s, exact Cancelled variant).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cx-cancel","distributed","http"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-l9mxa","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:58.856854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-lapw8","title":"Restore mux split wire JSON proptests dropped by 193a9ebb","description":"Commit 193a9ebb ("frankenterm-gui: saturate resize increment geometry") unintentionally deleted unrelated mux wire roundtrip coverage from frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs. Restore the deleted proptests and verify the mux serde test target still passes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:31.861035Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:38.190544Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:38.190265Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in HEAD via commit a3409685 (2026-04-20). The test file has a pre-existing compile issue (PaneNode: Clone trait bound + 15-tuple prop_map) that is unrelated to the 193a9ebb deletion; filing that separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-m1zkw","title":"[MEDIUM] mux PaneNode serde proptests omit the recursive Split variant","description":"Audit finding from commits 095ca88a + ccaa7385.

Files:
- frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:108-113
- frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:3907-3914

`PaneNode` is a recursive wire type used directly by the codec and it has three variants: `Empty`, `Leaf`, and `Split { left, right, node }`. The current generator only produces `Empty` and `Leaf`:

```rust
fn arb_pane_node() -> impl Strategy<Value = PaneNode> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(PaneNode::Empty),
        arb_pane_entry().prop_map(PaneNode::Leaf),
    ]
}
```

So `pane_node_json_roundtrip` never exercises the recursive split-tree encoding path. The follow-up commit ccaa7385 added scalar roundtrips for `SplitDirection` and `SplitSize`, but that still does not cover nested `PaneNode::Split` layouts or the interaction with `SplitDirectionAndSize`.

Expected fix:
- make `arb_pane_node()` recursive and generate bounded split trees
- assert roundtrip for at least one non-trivial nested split shape

Regression risk: codec-facing tab topology serde can regress on split trees while the current proptests stay green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:43.315765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T17:59:30.892198Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T17:59:30.891891Z","close_reason":"Already resolved. frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:99-118 now defines arb_pane_node via prop_recursive(depth=3, nodes=16, children=2, ...) that generates PaneNode::Split{left, right, node} at every level, with arb_split_direction_and_size feeding the split metadata. pane_node_json_roundtrip passes (verified: 'test pane_node_json_roundtrip ... ok' in /tmp/ft-pane6-target run against HEAD). Sibling tests layout_arrangement_json_roundtrip and swap_layout_json_roundtrip are currently failing on an unrelated f64 ratio roundtrip precision issue (e.g. 0.10814827875804041 vs 0.1081482787580404); filing that separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","mux","review","serde","test-gap"]}
-{"id":"ft-mdaon","title":"[MEDIUM] escape-parser robustness suite contains vacuous CSI MR and missing concatenation property","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit f3242133 (test: proptest robustness suite for escape-parser) overstates the protection added by frankenterm/escape-parser/tests/proptest_parser_robustness.rs.

Evidence:
- The file header claims property 3: `Concatenation consistency — parse(a++b) == parse(a) ++ parse(b)` (lines 9-10), but there is no corresponding test anywhere in the file. The suite advertises a metamorphic relation it never checks.
- The new `csi_roundtrip_via_display` property is partially vacuous (lines 170-184). It only asserts equality when `actions1.len() == 1` AND `actions2.len() == 1`; otherwise it silently skips the case. A regression that starts emitting zero or multiple actions for generated CSI sequences would therefore pass this property instead of failing.
- The generated inputs in `arb_csi_sequence()` are always syntactically complete CSI byte strings (lines 30-47), so a non-singleton parse result is exactly the kind of behavioral drift this MR should catch, not skip.

Why this matters:
- This commit is framed as a robustness/metamorphic suite, but two of the strongest claimed relations are currently absent or weak enough to miss structural parser regressions.
- Escape parsing is a streaming state machine; vacuous MRs give false confidence around output-shape stability.

Requested fix:
- Add the missing concatenation-consistency property or remove the claim from the suite header/commit description.
- Tighten `csi_roundtrip_via_display` so generated complete CSI inputs must roundtrip to exactly one action on both parses, and fail otherwise.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:56:59.004870Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:09:01.630039Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:09:01.626809Z","close_reason":"Tightened the CSI roundtrip metamorphic relation to a canonical singleton-CSI corpus and added the missing printable concatenation property; targeted escape-parser robustness test target now passes with the required clang env.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

`PaneNode` is a recursive wire type used directly by the codec and it has three variants: `Empty`, `Leaf`, and `Split { left, right, node }`. The current generator only produces `Empty` and `Leaf`:

```rust
fn arb_pane_node() -> impl Strategy<Value = PaneNode> {
```

So `pane_node_json_roundtrip` never exercises the recursive split-tree encoding path. The follow-up commit ccaa7385 added scalar roundtrips for `SplitDirection` and `SplitSize`, but that still does not cover nested `PaneNode::Split` layouts or the interaction with `SplitDirectionAndSize`.

Regression risk: codec-facing tab topology serde can regress on split trees while the current proptests stay green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:43.315765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:27.500875Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:27.500567Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by fef393d1 (ft-n38nt) — arb_pane_node() now uses prop_recursive(3, 16, 2) generating bounded split trees, plus nested split shape fixture test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","mux","review","serde","test-gap"]}
+{"id":"ft-mdaon","title":"[MEDIUM] escape-parser robustness suite contains vacuous CSI MR and missing concatenation property","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit f3242133 (test: proptest robustness suite for escape-parser) overstates the protection added by frankenterm/escape-parser/tests/proptest_parser_robustness.rs.

Evidence:
- The file header claims property 3: `Concatenation consistency — parse(a++b) == parse(a) ++ parse(b)` (lines 9-10), but there is no corresponding test anywhere in the file. The suite advertises a metamorphic relation it never checks.
- The new `csi_roundtrip_via_display` property is partially vacuous (lines 170-184). It only asserts equality when `actions1.len() == 1` AND `actions2.len() == 1`; otherwise it silently skips the case. A regression that starts emitting zero or multiple actions for generated CSI sequences would therefore pass this property instead of failing.
- The generated inputs in `arb_csi_sequence()` are always syntactically complete CSI byte strings (lines 30-47), so a non-singleton parse result is exactly the kind of behavioral drift this MR should catch, not skip.

Why this matters:
- This commit is framed as a robustness/metamorphic suite, but two of the strongest claimed relations are currently absent or weak enough to miss structural parser regressions.
- Escape parsing is a streaming state machine; vacuous MRs give false confidence around output-shape stability.

Requested fix:
- Add the missing concatenation-consistency property or remove the claim from the suite header/commit description.
- Tighten `csi_roundtrip_via_display` so generated complete CSI inputs must roundtrip to exactly one action on both parses, and fail otherwise.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:56:59.004870Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:11:19.890939Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:11:19.890629Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by db835e7b — csi_roundtrip_via_display tightened with arb_singleton_csi_sequence + hard asserts, concatenation_consistency_for_printable added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-mejto","title":"MCP tool/resource manifest golden — protocol conformance freeze","description":"Freeze the MCP server's public protocol surface via a golden manifest test: tool names + description/input-schema hashes, resource URIs + description hashes, resource-template URIs + description hashes.  {"id":"ft-n2j5w","title":"Fix MCP module pre-existing test errors under default features and verify 170 test functions pass","description":"# Goal
Fix the known test compilation/runtime errors in mcp.rs that prevent the 170 test functions from running with default features enabled.

# Background
MEMORY.md documents: mcp.rs has pre-existing test errors with default features. The MCP module (3,229 lines, 170 #[test] functions) has comprehensive test coverage. MCP submodules (mcp_bridge, mcp_framework, mcp_helpers, etc.) are declared inside mcp.rs via #[path], NOT in lib.rs — this means feature-gate interactions may affect module resolution.

# Design
## Diagnostic steps
1. Run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp 2>&1 | tee /tmp/mcp_errors.log
   (Using build recipe: CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<agent>-target)
2. Categorize errors:
   - Type mismatches from recent module changes -> update test expectations
   - Missing types/imports due to feature gates -> add #[cfg(feature)] or fix import paths
   - Async runtime errors (#[tokio::test] vs asupersync) -> migrate to sync or runtime_compat
   - Stale test data referencing old API shapes -> update fixtures
3. Fix each error, verifying after each fix that the change compiles
4. Final run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp -- all 170 tests green

## Common failure patterns to watch for
- MCP submodules use #[path] declarations — if a submodule file moves or renames, the path attribute breaks
- The 57 tokio-based test failures (ft-22x4r) may overlap — if MCP tests use #[tokio::test], coordinate with ft-682n6
- Feature flag interactions: some test helper types may only exist under specific features (mcp, subprocess-bridge, etc.)
- robot_types.rs changes may have updated MCP response envelope shapes without updating mcp.rs test expectations

## Structured Logging
- Diagnostic log: mcp_test_fix_<timestamp>.log with each error, category, fix applied
- Before/after test counts to verify no tests lost

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp passes with 0 failures under default features.
2. All 170 test functions execute (none skipped, none #[ignore]d without documented reason).
3. No semantic changes to test intent — only update expectations to match current API.
4. Diagnostic log documenting each fix for future reference.
5. If any tests require non-default features, they must have explicit #[cfg(feature = "...")] annotations with comments explaining why.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:08.516219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.705038Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.704865Z","close_reason":"Verified: 201 MCP tests pass with zero failures under default features (cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp::tests). The pre-existing test errors documented in memory were likely fixed by other agents. No code changes needed — the 170 test functions mentioned in the bead have grown to 201, all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fix","mcp","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-n38nt","title":"mux proptest_tab_wire_serde test target fails to compile (PaneNode: Clone + 15-tuple prop_map)","description":"The `cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml --test proptest_tab_wire_serde` target currently fails to compile at HEAD with two independent errors:

1. `error[E0277]: the trait bound PaneNode: Clone is not satisfied` at proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:108-111 — `arb_pane_node`'s `Just(PaneNode::Empty)` and `arb_pane_entry().prop_map(PaneNode::Leaf)` require `PaneNode: Clone` for proptest's `Strategy` impl. `PaneNode` only derives `Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq, Debug` at frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:3906.

2. `error[E0599]: the method `prop_map` exists for tuple (..., × 15), but its trait bounds were not satisfied` at proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:68 — `arb_pane_entry` builds a 15-element tuple but proptest 1.11's `Strategy` impl only extends to some smaller arity.

Both errors exist independently of ft-lapw8's split-test restoration. The test file has therefore been silently broken since at least commit 095ca88a. Surfacing this so the proptest suite is actually run in CI rather than silently failing to build.

Fix direction:
- Add `Clone` to `PaneNode` derive. `PaneEntry`, `SplitDirectionAndSize`, `SplitDirection`, `SplitSize`, and `Box<PaneNode>` already support Clone — the transitive closure is clean.
- Refactor `arb_pane_entry`'s 15-element tuple into a nested tuple (`((a,b,c,d,e,f,g), (h,i,j,k,l,m,n,o))`) or a fixture struct + `prop_map_each` to stay within proptest's tuple-arity limit.

Evidence: cargo test output at audit time shows 3 errors (8 error spans) under --no-default-features defaults with `CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane6-target`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:20:56.034014Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:30:39.493294Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:30:39.493003Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","compile","mux","proptest","test-gap"]}
